title: Slagline Cant
atAGlance: Slagline Cant is a clipped worker slang used by lift crews, forge hands, and sentries to communicate quickly across noise, smoke, and crowd panic.
sageNotes: Cant began as practical shorthand shouted over hammerfall, then spread into taverns, contracts, and even court testimony when judges needed plain speech. A phrase like 'three down, ash high' can mean weather, casualties, and route danger in a single breath depending on tone. Nobles mock it until they need a floor boss to explain why their caravan is stranded. If someone tells you 'rope honest, bell false,' trust the rope and find a new commander.
gameInformation: Use as social texture and a lightweight code mechanic. PCs fluent in Slagline can parse hidden warnings, detect forged orders, or gain advantage in worker districts. Quick table seeds: slang misunderstandings causing duels, coded graffiti marking safe alleys, or a witness who will only testify in cant before guild elders.
